title: Discrepancy between self-report and neurophysiological markers of socio-affective responses in lonely individuals
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 148 neurologically healthy adults performing an emotion processing and regulation task with static visual stimuli. Participants completed 240 trials in a 2×3 factorial design manipulating stimulus content (social vs. non-social) and regulation requirement (passive viewing of neutral or negative images, or cognitive reappraisal of negative images), with subsequent subjective ratings of arousal and valence. The study investigates discrepancies between self-reported and neurophysiological markers of socio-affective responses in lonely individuals.
methods_description: EEG was recorded from 64 channels using a QuickCap and Neuroscan SynampsRT amplifier at 1000 Hz sampling rate with impedance maintained below 5 kΩ. Additional physiological signals included horizontal and vertical electrooculography, electrocardiography, and galvanic skin response. Preprocessing in MATLAB using EEGLAB and ERPLAB included band-pass filtering (0.1–30 Hz), downsampling to 250 Hz, average mastoid re-referencing, automatic bad-channel rejection, independent component analysis with ADJUST and SASICA for component rejection, spherical interpolation, epoching (−200 to 5000 ms), baseline correction, and artifact rejection (±100 µV threshold).
